div1.entryFree: Nerve, that in which the strength of a body lies, rather == sinew, tendon, than an organ of sensation and motion: needs must I under my transgression bow, unless my --s were brass, Sonn. 120, 4. thy --s are in their infancy again and have no vigour in them, Tp. I, 2, 484. the strongest --s and small inferior veins from me receive that natural competency, Cor. I, 1, 142. my firm --s shall never tremble, Mcb. III, 4, 102. makes each petty artery in this body as hardy as the Nemean lion's n. Hml. I, 4, 83. a brain that nourishes our --s, and can get goal for goal of youth, Ant. IV, 8, 21. strains his young --s and puts himself in posture, Cymb. III, 3, 94. Metaphorically: those that know the very --s of state, Meas. I, 4, 53. thou great commander, n. and bone of Greece, Troil. I, 3, 55.
